Front

hitman

A person paid to kill someone; a professional murderer

"Joe worked as a specialized hitman known as a looper"

incinerator

A furnace for burning waste; used to destroy bodies

"...take the body to an incinerator to dispose of all evidence"

executioner

A person who carries out a death sentence

"...recruited several young men to act as executioners"

syndicate

An organized group of criminals

"the five largest criminal syndicates in the future"

torture

inflict severe pain on someone

"his younger version was being tortured by the organization"

destined

Meant to happen in the future; predetermined

"destined to be shot by their younger self"

cease

To stop or come to an end

"he would cease to exist"

vanish

To disappear suddenly or completely

"making the victims vanish forever"

telekinesis

The ability to move objects using only the mind

"born with a unique ability called telekinesis"

evolution

Gradual development; change over time

"evolution in that year had made 10% of the world's population born with these natural talents"

trauma

A psychological shock or painful experience

"older Joe would give Sid trauma and hatred"

sympathy

Feelings of pity and sorrow for someone

"caused him to feel unexpected sympathy"

haunted

Preoccupied with troubling thoughts

"these thoughts haunted him"

determined

Having made a firm decision

"remained determined to kill him"

paradox

A seemingly contradictory situation

(killing one's future self)

inevitable

Unavoidable; certain to happen

"whether it happened tomorrow or next month"

consequence

A result or effect of an action

actions ripple through time

dispose of

Phrasal Verb
To get rid of something; to eliminate

"...killers dispose of bodies by sending them back in time"

send back

Phrasal Verb
To return something or someone to a previous place or time

"...targets sent back from the future"

track down

Phrasal Verb
To find someone or something after searching

"older Joe had tracked down them"

hunt down

Phrasal Verb
To pursue and capture/kill someone

"was hunting down every retired looper"

side with

Phrasal Verb
To support or align with someone

"chose to side with the organization"

close the loop

Idiom
To complete a cycle; in this story, to kill one's future self

"This process was known as closing the loop"

connect the dots

Idiom
To understand the relationship between different things

"finally connected the dots"

break the cycle

Idiom
To end a repeating pattern

"to break this endless cycle"

face to face

Idiom
In person; meeting directly

"first time they sat face to face"

cease to exist

Expression
To stop being; to no longer exist

"would immediately cease to exist"

This caused...

IELTS Band 7.0+
Cause and Effect marker

"This caused only secret criminal organizations to continue to use the technology"

Because of this...

IELTS Band 7.0+
Result link marker

"Because of this, it was nearly impossible to simply bury a body"

This resulted in...

IELTS Band 8.0+
Formal result marker

implied usage

However

IELTS Band 7.0+
Standard contrast marker

"However, because the government and society in the future did not approve..."

Although

IELTS Band 7.0+
Concession clause marker

"Although it felt like a heavy burden..."

Despite

IELTS Band 7.5+
Prepositional concession

"Despite this risk, people like Joe...were willing to perform this task"

On the other hand

IELTS Band 7.5+
Contrastive phrase

"On the other hand, back at the headquarters..."

At the same time

IELTS Band 7.0+
Simultaneity marker

"At the same time, the world Joe lived in did not yet possess the technology"

Meanwhile

IELTS Band 7.0+
Simultaneous action marker

"Meanwhile, the older version of Seth, who was still running away..."

By the time

IELTS Band 7.0+
Temporal boundary

"By the time he arrived, he had lost both of his legs"

Eventually

IELTS Band 7.0+
Final outcome marker

"he eventually received the task to kill his own future self"

What year did the story take place in?

Plot Detail

2044 (Kansas)

What was a "looper"?

Plot Detail

A specialized hitman who killed targets sent back from the future

What were targets paid with?

Plot Detail

Silver bars (regular targets) or Gold bars (closing the loop)

When did time travel become possible?

Plot Detail

2074 (30 years after 2044)

Who led the Looper organization?

Character

Abe (a criminal leader from the future)

What was "closing the loop"?

Plot Concept

Killing one's future self to eliminate witnesses

What special ability did Seth have?

Character Ability

Telekinesis (moving objects with his mind)

Who was the Rain Maker?

Character/Villain

A mysterious figure who took over criminal syndicates and hunted retired loopers

Who was Sid living with?

Character Detail

Sarah (his mother) on a farm

How did young Joe save the future?

Plot Resolution

By shooting himself, making older Joe vanish and preventing Sid's trauma

What percentage of people had telekinetic abilities?

World Building

10% of the population

Why was crime impossible in 2074?

World Building

Tracking technology was so advanced that even the smallest evidence could be discovered

Why did Seth fail to close his loop?

Plot Point

His older self sang and told stories, making him feel sympathy and lose concentration

What happened when young Seth was tortured?

Plot Point

His older self began losing body parts (fingers, nose, legs)

Why did older Joe travel back to 2044?

Character Motivation

To kill the Rain Maker as a child and save his wife

Why were bodies sent back in time?

Plot Explanation

Because tracking technology in the future was too advanced to hide crimes

What powers did Sid have?

Character Ability

Extremely powerful telekinesis - stronger than anyone else's

How did young Joe break the endless loop?

Plot Resolution

By shooting himself, causing older Joe to cease to exist

Theme: Fate vs. Free Will

Thematic Discussion

Can we change our destiny, or is our future already written? The story explores whether knowing the future allows us to change it.

Theme: Cause and Effect

Thematic Discussion

Actions in the present ripple through time. Older Joe's attempt to save his wife creates the very future he fears.

Theme: Moral Responsibility

Thematic Discussion

Are we responsible for creating the monsters we fear? The story suggests older Joe creates the Rain Maker through his actions.

Theme: Sacrifice

Thematic Discussion

Sometimes breaking a cycle requires personal sacrifice. Young Joe gives his life to save Sarah and Sid.

Theme: Identity

Thematic Discussion

What connects our past, present, and future selves? The story explores how different versions of the same person interact.

Why did loopers agree to close their loops?

Discussion Question

Possible answers: the gold bars (wealth), belief they could change their fate, reckless lifestyle, lack of other options

Did young Joe make the right decision at the end?

Discussion Question

Open-ended: Discuss sacrifice, breaking cycles, moral responsibility, selflessness vs. self-preservation

Is older Joe a villain or a victim?

Discussion Question

Open-ended: Discuss his motivations (love for wife), actions (killing children), moral complexity

What happened to Sid and Sarah after the story?

Speculation

Open-ended: Did Sid avoid becoming the Rain Maker? How did they live?

Execute a complete sentence using "hitman"

Sentence Production

Example: The hitman was paid to eliminate the target without leaving any evidence.

Execute a complete sentence using "cease"

Sentence Production

Example: Without funding, the project would cease to exist within months.

Execute a complete sentence using "vanish"

Sentence Production

Example: The magician made the rabbit vanish from the hat in seconds.

Execute a complete sentence using "dispose of"

Sentence Production

Example: You must properly dispose of hazardous materials according to regulations.

Execute a complete sentence using "track down"

Sentence Production

Example: The investigator managed to track down the witness after weeks of searching.

Execute a complete sentence using "side with"

Sentence Production

Example: Despite the pressure, she refused to side with either faction in the dispute.

Execute a complete sentence using "destined"

Sentence Production

Example: Many believed he was destined for greatness from a young age.

Execute a complete sentence using "trauma"

Sentence Production

Example: The accident left her with psychological trauma that took years to overcome.

Execute a complete sentence using "hunt down"

Sentence Production

Example: The authorities vowed to hunt down those responsible for the crime.

Execute a complete sentence using "figure out"

Sentence Production

Example: It took us hours to figure out the solution to the puzzle.

Summarize the story in one sentence.

Comprehension

A hitman named Joe must confront his future self who has traveled back in time to kill a child who will become a dictator, leading to a sacrifice that breaks the cycle of violence.

What is the central conflict of the story?

Analysis

Man vs. Self (young vs. old Joe), Man vs. Fate (changing destiny), Man vs. Society (against the criminal organization)

How does the story use time travel as a metaphor?

Analysis

Time travel represents how our past actions affect our future, and how confronting our past selves is necessary for growth.

What role does trauma play in the story?

Analysis

Trauma is shown as the cause of the Rain Maker's transformation, suggesting that violence creates more violence.
